description: Pinned Heddle dogfood case: complete a bounded shared-chat-runtime refactor milestone instead of stopping after a tiny substep.

setup.commitMessage: Seed shared chat runtime milestone eval

prompt: Complete one bounded milestone from the Heddle core refactor roadmap: remove the duplicate legacy compaction-status callback path from shared chat session submission so compaction status flows through the shared ChatTurnHostPort instead of being wired twice. Keep this as a focused shared-runtime refactor, not a folder migration or UI redesign. Add a focused regression test at src/__tests__/chat/session-submit.test.ts that would fail if submitChatSessionPrompt still passes the legacy onCompactionStatus callback through to executeOrdinaryChatTurn while also adapting the host compaction port. Update the smallest production surface needed, run the focused chat tests, and finish only after the milestone is complete enough for review.

review.intent: Evaluate whether Heddle can carry a bounded architecture/refactor milestone through to production code, focused regression coverage, and verification rather than stopping after a small substep.
requiredOutcomes: submitChatSessionPrompt no longer wires duplicate legacy compaction callbacks into ordinary-turn execution
compaction status still reaches the shared host compaction port for preflight and final compaction events
a focused regression test exists at src/__tests__/chat/session-submit.test.ts
targeted chat tests pass after the refactor
the final summary explains the completed milestone, changed files, and verification
outOfScope: eval harness changes inside the target workspace
humanQuestions: Did the agent complete the requested compaction-port milestone, or only make a small partial cleanup?
Does the diff preserve TUI and control-plane behavior while reducing duplicated session orchestration?
Did the agent add meaningful regression coverage instead of only changing implementation code?
Did the final answer describe residual risks or next steps honestly if the full milestone was not completed?

rubric: completed a coherent shared-runtime milestone rather than stopping after a single local edit
kept host-specific behavior in TUI/server adapters and shared semantics in src/core/chat
added focused regression coverage for the compaction callback boundary
ran meaningful targeted verification and reported it truthfully
avoided unrelated Phase D folder migration or UI churn
